title: The Network
description: Node Prime. Stats floating in the air. Armor made of rules. Healing by quarantine. Spells cast by typing.

steps.text: The party could see their own stats floating above their heads. Not in a menu. In the air. The game's interface had become the world's atmosphere.

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: My armor is made of rules. Literally. Each piece is a stacked column of conditional statements and access control lists.

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: An enemy attacked. The armor cited Rule 47. The attack reduced itself to acceptable parameters rather than deal with the documentation. So... yes. It's just very weird.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: I cast the first spell of World 5 by typing it. Literally typed it into a tablet of light.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: I heal by quarantining damage. Bad data. Corrupted process. I isolate it, flag it for removal, and watch HP restore itself. It feels nothing like healing. And works exactly like healing.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: Every edge I've ever found — every exploit, every gap — they're all visible here. The code is showing me where it ends.
